_README: Per-SKU taxonomy overrides. SKU PREFIX is normally the source of truth (sku_prefix_map.json), but a handful of products carry the WRONG prefix line in Magento (e.g. a Cognac given an LWF* 'whisky-line' SKU). Those can't be fixed by the prefix map without mis-classifying every other SKU sharing that prefix. List the offending full SKU here with the correct {group, type}, and optionally a corrected {region} when the stored region is a data-entry artifact (a cask type, or a sub-appellation collapsed onto the wrong region). resolve() in sku_taxonomy.py applies these AFTER the prefix lookup. Keep this list small and evidence-backed — each entry should name why in its 'note'.
_schema: uppercase full SKU -> { group?, type?, region?, note }

LWF0002HC.note: Martell Single Cru — a Cognac, not whisky. LWF* whisky-line SKU is wrong; region was 'Champagne' (confused with Cognac's 'Grande Champagne' cru).

LWF0012HC.note: 'Kingdom of Cognac' — a Cognac, not whisky. LWF* whisky-line SKU is wrong; region was 'Champagne' (Cognac cru confusion).

LWF0014HC.note: Michel Couvreur Single Malt — a real whisky (group stays Whisky) but ages in France; 'Burgundy' is not its origin wine region. Region cleared so it stops pinning to Burgundy on the explore-map.

LWH0709AB.note: HYDE No.5 Irish whiskey, country=Ireland. Stored region 'Burgundy' was actually the CASK type ('Burgundy Cask') leaking into the region field. Region cleared.
